text: You read a poem about someone who chose to stay. About all the moments where leaving would have been easier — and something quieter than faith made them sit back down.
Grubwin is very still while you read. His face does something complicated.
After the exchange, during tea, Grubwin comes to stand beside you. He doesn't say anything for a moment.

speaker: Grubwin
text: I didn't realize it was that visible. The staying.

speaker: [player]
text: It was kind of the most visible thing about you.
He looks at the worm poster. You both look at the worm poster.

speaker: Grubwin
text: I'm glad you're here. For what it's worth.
